claimText: The Malabar Grey Hornbill is endemic to the Western Ghats of India.
evidenceExcerpt: Endemic to Western Ghats of India.
examUse: Statement-based question on species distribution and endemism.
distractorHooks: Endemic to Andaman and Nicobar Islands

claimText: Unlike typical hornbill species, the Malabar Grey Hornbill lacks a casque.
evidenceExcerpt: Lacks the casque typical of Hornbill
distractorHooks: Possesses a prominent, brightly colored casque
Is the only hornbill with a double casque

claimText: The Malabar Grey Hornbill is listed under Schedule I of the Wildlife Protection Act (WPA), 1972.
examUse: Statement-based question on legal protection status of Indian bird species.

claimText: The IUCN Red List status of the Malabar Grey Hornbill is Vulnerable (VU).
examUse: Correct-pair question matching species to their IUCN status.
